item.title: The Forbidden Alloy of WW2
item.author: Real Engineering

item.title: The Killer Who Tried to Become Dexter
item.author: Weird History
item.content:encoded:
The disturbing phenomenon of copycat killers has blurred the line between entertainment and reality for decades. In one of the most chilling true crime cases, Mark Andrew Twitchell, an aspiring serial killer from Canada, became obsessed with the hit TV series Dexter, modeling his crimes after television's infamous vigilante.
On this episode of Weird History Presents: Graveyard Shift, we take a look at the bizarre and horrifying story of how a TV antihero influenced a real-life killer, the shocking evidence investigators uncovered, and why the case became one of the most infamous examples of life imitating art.
Are you a fan of Dexter? Let us know in the comments.
Be sure to subscribe to the Weird History Newsletter: https://bit.ly/WeirdHistoryNews
#dexter #truecrimestories #weirdhistory

item.title: This isn’t the original Statue of Liberty
item.author: Real Engineering

item.title: Psychedelics, mind control, radiation-eating: The secret superpowers of fungi - Max G. Levy
item.author: TED-Ed
item.content:encoded:
Dig into the vast and wild world of fungi, and explore how these organisms evolved to play a range of essential ecological roles.
--
The diverse fungal kingdom, which includes everything from baker’s yeast to edible mushrooms to foot infections, has long defied assumptions. Scientists estimate that somewhere between 2 and 12 million species of fungi remain undiscovered— either because they’re microscopic, live hidden within other species, or for other unknown reasons. Max G. Levy digs into the vast and wild world of fungi.
Lesson by Max G. Levy, directed by Petya Zlateva, Compote Collective.
Support Our Non-Profit Mission ---------------------------------------------- Support us on Patreon: http://bit.ly/TEDEdPatreon Check out our merch: http://bit.ly/TEDEDShop ----------------------------------------------

item.title: Something Is Happening Under Pluto's Surface
item.author: Astrum
item.content:encoded:
Will Pluto ever be a planet again? Check out Ouranos with this link to find the best stargazing conditions and make the most of your next observing session: http://ouranos-app.com/download - start with a 7 day-free trial or grab 30% off an annual subscription
▀▀▀▀▀▀
This Astrum supercut explores our favourite dwarf planet: Pluto. We’ll discover why its planetary status really got revoked, take a closer look at what’s hiding under the surface, and reveal the surprising discoveries on its largest moon, Charon.
To those returning and new to the channel: This video is a supercut of some of Astrum’s best Pluto videos, plus new and updated content. We’ve edited this into a new seamless video, remastered in 4K resolution.
▀▀▀▀▀▀
0:00 Pluto 2:05 New Horizons Images 4:55 Pluto’s Heart 9:21 Subsurface Ocean 11:25 Cryovolcanoes 13:03 Charon 17:29 Double Planet 20:53 What Is a Planet? 23:14 The Kiss and Capture Theory 26:20 Charon’s Surface 29:36 Cryovolcanoes on Charon 31:55 Mordor Macula: Charon’s Red Spot 35:56 Will Pluto Be a Planet Again?
▀▀▀▀▀▀
To stay on top of space news, sign up to the Astrum newsletter: https://astrumspace.kit.com

References: “Could Pluto Become a Planet Again?”, via bbc.co.uk https://astrumspace.info/plutoplanet “New Horizons Mission”, via science.nasa.gov https://astrumspace.info/newhorizons “New Horizons Images of Pluto”, via nasa.gov https://astrumspace.info/nh_pluto “What Defines a Planet”, via open.edu https://astrumspace.info/planetdef “Charon: Pluto’s Largest Moon”, via nasa.gov https://astrumspace.info/charonoverview “The Accidental Discovery of Charon”, via nasa.gov https://astrumspace.info/charondiscovery “The Dynamics of Pluto’s Satellites”, via iop.org https://astrumspace.info/plutosatellites “The Pluto-Charon System (NASA Lithograph)”, via nasa.gov https://astrumspace.info/plutocharon “The ‘Kiss-and-Capture’ Origin of Pluto’s Moon Charon”, via nature.com https://astrumspace.info/kisscapture “Differentiation and Cryovolcanism in the Pluto–Charon System”, via usra.edu https://astrumspace.info/charoncryovolc

#Astrum #Space #Pluto

item.title: Why The German Army Feared The Night Witches
item.author: Weird History
item.content:encoded:
On the dark battlefields of World War II, German soldiers feared an enemy they could barely see and rarely heard... until it was too late. Out of the blackness came a faint whooshing sound. Seconds later, bombs rained from the sky, buildings crumbled, and chaos erupted across the Eastern Front. On this episode of Weird History we take a look at the mysterious Night Witches: an elite squadron of all-female Soviet pilots who became one of the most feared forces of World War II. Flying outdated wooden biplanes on daring nighttime bombing missions, they terrorized German troops, earned countless military honors, and cemented their place among history's most legendary warriors. Have you ever heard of the Night Witches? Let us know in the comments.
(Much like real history, this video was produced entirely by human beings.)
Be sure to subscribe to the Weird History Newsletter: https://bit.ly/WeirdHistoryNews
#wwii #nightwitches #weirdhistory

item.title: The Truth about Space Data Centers
item.author: Real Engineering
item.content:encoded:

item.title: The Strangest Medical Stories We Could Find | Compilation
item.author: Weird History
item.content:encoded:
The History of Medicine is a wild one. How humans figured out cures and even developed surgical procedures that saved lives! Often times what seemed to work in the past was later found to be detrimental to health and then abandoned. Today on Weird History we go into various aspects of the history of Medicine, the good, the bad, and the out-right miraculous! What is your favorite part about the history of all things medical? Let us know in the comments!
Be sure to subscribe to the Weird History Newsletter: https://bit.ly/WeirdHistoryNews
Chapters: 00:00:00 - What It Was Like Going To A Doctor In Wild West 00:10:11 - What It's Like to Be In an Iron Lung 00:21:34 - Bizarre and Bloody Practices of Medieval Barbers 00:33:22 - A Doctor Tried to Resurrect George Washington From the Dead 00:45:52 - What It's Actually Like To Have A Lobotomy 00:57:04 - The Crazy True Story Of The First Plastic Surgery In History 01:07:13 - Walter Jackson Freeman II The Champion Of The Lobotomy 01:22:33 - Horrifying Things We Used To Use As Medicine
#medicine #compilation #weirdhistory

item.title: How a broken heart affects your body - Roni Shanoada
item.author: TED-Ed
item.content:encoded:
Dig into broken heart syndrome, and find out how grief and traumatic events can affect your body and health.
--
Losing a loved one can be one of the most traumatic experiences of a person’s life. Grief brings on a whole host of strong emotions that impact both our mental and physical health. But is it possible for these powerful feelings to endanger your life? In other words, can you actually die from a broken heart? Roni Shanoada explores how traumatic events can affect your body.
Lesson by Roni Shanoada, directed by Joana Sus, NOMINT.
Support Our Non-Profit Mission ---------------------------------------------- Support us on Patreon: http://bit.ly/TEDEdPatreon Check out our merch: http://bit.ly/TEDEDShop ----------------------------------------------

item.title: This Moon Collision Did Something Crazy on Earth
item.author: Astrum
item.content:encoded:
What created Tycho crater? Get a 10% discount on your next VisiSolar purchase when you use code ASTRUM2026 or the following link: https://visisolar.com/discount/ASTRUM2026
▀▀▀▀▀▀
Our Moon is covered with scars, but one is more recognisable than any other: Tycho. Formed in a violent impact that sent debris flying toward Earth, this iconic crater holds clues to the Moon’s hidden secrets. In this video, we’re digging into the evidence to discover what collided with the Moon, what happened here on Earth, and why the timeline still puzzles scientists today.
▀▀▀▀▀▀
0:00 Tycho Crater 2:24 Central Mountain 4:45 Lava Tsunami 7:43 Aftermath 8:39 Landslide 10:59 Raining Glass 12:03 Seen From Earth 13:08 Raining Glass on Earth 13:57 How Old Is Tycho? 15:55 Fresh Crater 17:17 Earth-Moon Exchange
▀▀▀▀▀▀

item.title: The Ghosts Of Alcatraz
item.author: Weird History
item.content:encoded:
Alcatraz, also known as "The Rock," was a maximum-security fortress where only the most dangerous and clever criminals were sent. One of the most infamous prisons in American history, it sat alone on a rocky island in San Francisco Bay and was once considered escape-proof. But decades after the prison closed, questions still linger. Did some prisoners actually escape? What secrets remain hidden behind its crumbling walls? And could Alcatraz be a place where even death isn't enough to set you free?
In this episode of Weird History Presents: Graveyard Shift we take a look at the strange history of Alcatraz, the prison that may never let go.
Be sure to subscribe to the Weird History Newsletter: https://bit.ly/WeirdHistoryNews
#haunted #alcatraz #weirdhistory

item.title: What causes avalanches, and can you survive them? - Simon Trautman
item.author: TED-Ed
item.content:encoded:
Explore the three conditions needed to trigger an avalanche, and what makes these natural disasters so hard to survive.
--
Avalanches are the rapid descent of snow, ice, rock, or a combination of these down a slope. They can be destructive natural disasters and personal tragedies: killing an average of 27 people annually in the US. These alpine nightmares don’t just awaken out of nowhere; they need three specific conditions. So, what causes an avalanche? And is there any way to survive? Simon Trautman investigates.
Lesson by Simon Trautman, directed by Denys Spolitak.
Support Our Non-Profit Mission ---------------------------------------------- Support us on Patreon: http://bit.ly/TEDEdPatreon Check out our merch: http://bit.ly/TEDEDShop ----------------------------------------------

item.title: $1 vs $50 Phone Charger. What’s the difference?
item.author: Real Engineering

item.title: Why This Princess's Life Was No Fairy Tale
item.author: Weird History
item.content:encoded:
Princess Diana was one of the most beloved and iconic royals in modern history. But behind the fairy-tale image, lies a far darker story. Long before the paparazzi, royal scandals, and the tragic Paris car crash that shocked the world, Diana's life was marked by loneliness, heartbreak, and immense pressure.
In this episode of Weird History, we're uncovering the true story of Princess Diana, from her troubled childhood and turbulent royal marriage to the events that made her a global icon and ultimately led to one of the most tragic endings in modern history.
Were you a fan of Princess Di? Let us know in the comments.
(Much like real history, this video was produced entirely by human beings.)
Be sure to subscribe to the Weird History Newsletter: https://bit.ly/WeirdHistoryNews
#princessdianaforever #britishmonarchy #weirdhistory

item.title: The Weird History of Sports and Games | Compilation
item.author: Weird History
item.content:encoded:
Sports, games, and entertainment of the masses have been part of the human experience since, well, humans existed! ...showcasing physical prowess, raw talent, or even taking time of a busy schedule for a board game or two. Join us as we get into all things sports, spectating, and many other things! For example, what was the average spectator experience at the old Roman Colosseum like? How did Milton Bradley start an incredible board game empire? ...and what was that whole Atari E.T. video game debacle all about? These and many more subjects in today's compilation - let us you your favorite game or sport in the comments!
Chapters
00:00:00 - What It Was Like to Be an Ancient Olympian 00:11:38 - What Being a Spectator at the Rome Colosseum Was Like 00:22:23 - How A Baseball Player Became a World War II Assassin 00:34:02 - The Family-Friendly Milton Bradley Corporation Has A Bizarre History 00:44:14 - How the ET Video Game Ended Atari 00:54:33 - When The Olympics Became A Hostage Crisis 01:04:39 - Facts About Bobby Fischer
Be sure to subscribe to the Weird History Newsletter: https://bit.ly/WeirdHistoryNews

item.title: Should you rinse your dishes before placing them in the dishwasher? - Rachel Yang
item.author: TED-Ed
item.content:encoded:
Take a look inside a dishwasher to explore how these machines work, and find out how to get the cleanest dishes from every use.
--
After years of hosting lavish dinner parties and watching her dishware get chipped during handwashing, American socialite Josephine Cochrane was convinced the world needed a better way to clean dishes. So, she built a prototype for a dishwashing contraption. And the design would become the model for our modern dishwashers. So, how do they work? Rachel Yang takes a look inside the machine.
Lesson by Rachel Yang, directed by Aaron Augenblick, Augenblick Studios.
Support Our Non-Profit Mission ---------------------------------------------- Support us on Patreon: http://bit.ly/TEDEdPatreon Check out our merch: http://bit.ly/TEDEDShop ----------------------------------------------

item.title: Sagittarius A* Might Not Be a Black Hole
item.author: Astrum
item.content:encoded:
A new theory suggests Sagittarius A* might not be a black hole after all. Start speaking a new language in 3 weeks with Babbel 🎉. Get up to 55% OFF ➡️ Here: https://bit.ly/AstrumJun26
▀▀▀▀▀▀
A new theory is challenging everything we know about Sagittarius A*, the supermassive object at the heart of our galaxy. For decades, physicists have been certain it’s a black hole. They have observed its effects and even built a planet-sized telescope just to image it. But now, a mind-bending theory is turning our understanding upside down. What if Sagittarius A* isn’t a black hole at all, but something far stranger?
▀▀▀▀▀▀
0:00 Sagittarius A* 1:44 Supermassive Black Hole 6:51 Not a Black Hole? 7:49 The Black Hole Problem 9:36 Dark Matter Core 11:23 What Is Dark Matter? 13:36 Dark Fermions 17:06 The Rotation Problem 18:49 Event Horizon Telescope 21:33 Photon Ring 23:16 More Evidence
▀▀▀▀▀▀
To stay on top of space news, sign up to the Astrum newsletter: https://astrumspace.kit.com
A huge thanks to our Patreons who help make these videos possible. Sign-up here to support the channel: https://bit.ly/4aiJZNF

References: “Imaging Fermionic Dark Matter Cores”, via oup.com https://astrumspace.info/fermiondm “Stars Orbiting a Dark Matter Compact Object”, via oup.com https://astrumspace.info/starsdm “Discovery of Sgr A*”, via arxiv.org https://astrumspace.info/sgra “Astronomers Reveal First Image of the Black Hole at the Heart of Our Galaxy”, via eventhorizontelescope.org https://astrumspace.info/ehtimage “Fast Moving Stars in S2 Orbit”, via iop.org https://astrumspace.info/s2orbit “The Nobel Prize in Physics 2020”, via nobelprize.org https://astrumspace.info/nobel2020 “Stellar Orbits Around Central Black Hole”, via arxiv.org https://astrumspace.info/stellarorbits “Elementary Fermions”, via nih.gov https://astrumspace.info/fermions “Keplerian Decline in the Milky Way Rotation Curve”, via arxiv.org https://astrumspace.info/keplerian “Collapse of Dark Matter Cores into Supermassive Black Holes”, via arxiv.org https://astrumspace.info/dmcollapse
▀▀▀▀▀▀
Credits: Writer: Patricia Ward Video Editor: Suhith Audio Editor: James Horsley Researcher: Shourya Shrivastava Script Editor: Damaris McColgan Thumbnail Designers: Peter Sheppard, Juane Dames Publishing Lead: Georgina Brenner Production Coordinator: Rose Unwin Edit Producer: Poppy Pinnock Head of Astrum: Jess Jordan Creator of Astrum: Alex McColgan
With special thanks to: Valentina Crespi Dr Carlos R Argüelles Institute of Astrophysics La Plata, Argentina NASA/ESO/ESA
#Astrum #Space #BlackHole

item.title: The Real Nightmare That Inspired Open Water
item.author: Weird History
item.content:encoded:
Imagine going on a 40-minute scuba dive in the middle of the ocean and then surfacing to discover your boat has vanished. You’ve been accidentally left behind, surrounded by endless open water with nowhere to go and no way to call for help. Panic sets in, and every minute becomes a fight for survival.
On this episode of Weird History Presents: Graveyard Shift we take a look at the terrifying true story of Tom and Eileen Lonergan, divers who were left stranded at sea and learned the hard way how a simple mistake can turn an ordinary scuba trip into a real-life nightmare.
Be sure to subscribe to the Weird History Newsletter: https://bit.ly/WeirdHistoryNews
#deepsea #horror #weirdhistory

item.title: How to attack (and defend) a medieval castle - Connor C. Wilson
item.author: TED-Ed
item.content:encoded:
Dig into the history of medieval European castles, and explore how the design of these formidable fortresses evolved over time.
--
Medieval European castles weren’t just for passive defense and withstanding enemy onslaughts; they were symbols of power, private residences, and hubs of governance. Castles regularly served as bases for launching attacks and as a way to control local populations. So, how were these formidable fortresses built? Connor C. Wilson explores how the design of these structures evolved over time.
Lesson by Connor C. Wilson, directed by Layron DeJarnette.
Support Our Non-Profit Mission ---------------------------------------------- Support us on Patreon: http://bit.ly/TEDEdPatreon Check out our merch: http://bit.ly/TEDEDShop ----------------------------------------------

Keep Learning ---------------------------------------------- View full lesson: https://ed.ted.com/lessons/how-to-attack-and-defend-a-medieval-castle-connor-c-wilson Dig deeper with additional resources: https://ed.ted.com/lessons/how-to-attack-and-defend-a-medieval-castle-connor-c-wilson/digdeeper

Thank you so much to our patrons for your support! Without you this video would not be possible! Po Foon Kwong, Boffin, Jesse Jurman, Scott Markley, Elija Peterson, Ovidiu Mrd, paul g mohney, Steven Razey, Nathan Giusti, Helen Lee, Anthony Benedict, Karthik Balsubramanian, Annastasshia Ames, Amy Lopez, Vinh-Thuy Nguyen, Liz Candee, Ugur Doga Sezgin, Karmi Nguyen, John C. Vesey, Yelena Baykova, Nick Johnson, Carlos H. Costa, Jennifer Kurkoski, Ryan B Harvey, Akinola Emmanuel, Jose Arcadio Valdes Franco, Sebastiaan Vleugels, Karl Laius, JY Kang, Abhishek Goel, Heidi Stolt, Nicole Sund, Karlee Finch, Mario Mejia, Denise A Pitts, Doug Henry, Keven Webb, Mihai Sandu, Deepak Iyer, Javid Gozalov, Kyanta Yap, Rebecca Reineke, William Biersdorf, Patricia Alves Panagides, Yvette Mocete, Cyrus Garay, Samuel Barbas, LadyGeek, Marin Kovachev, and Penelope Misquitta.

item.title: Why Elvis Wanted The Beatles OUT Of America
item.author: Weird History
item.content:encoded:
To the four young musicians from Liverpool who would become The Beatles, Elvis Presley was more than just the King of Rock ’n’ Roll, he was their hero. But the feeling was far from mutual.
Behind the scenes, Elvis grew increasingly bitter toward the band he inspired and what began as mutual admiration turned into resentment, jealousy, and even outright hostility.
On this episode of Weird History, we're looking at why Elvis Presley hated The Beatles and what happened when the world's biggest music icon was forced to watch a new generation take his throne. Which side of this rock n roll feud do you come down on? Let us know in the comments.
Be sure to subscribe to the Weird History Newsletter: https://bit.ly/WeirdHistoryNews
#elvis #beatles #weirdhistory

item.title: How Tragedy Shaped History | Compilation
item.author: Weird History
item.content:encoded:
Life, and the human experience is FULL of ups and downs through history. Today on Weird History we are looking at some on the tragic side of life - What exactly happened to the Space Shuttle Columbia? Why did the Concorde get discontinues? ... and what happened behind the scenes on the filming of the Twilight Zone, which lead to the tragic deaths on set? join us as we go through these and other stories from the past.... what is a tragic situation you remember? Let us know in the comments!
Be sure to subscribe to the Weird History Newsletter: https://bit.ly/WeirdHistoryNews
Chapters:
00:00:00 - Space Shuttle Columbia 00:10:24 - The Rise and Fall of the Concorde 00:22:40 - Mount Everest Frozen Man 00:32:28 - Twilight Zone 00:43:24 - Magic Tricks That Backfired
#tragedy #compilation #weirdhistory

item.title: Meet the slow loris, the only venomous primate in existence - Stephanie A. Poindexter
item.author: TED-Ed
item.content:encoded:
Get to know the slow loris, the world's only venomous primate, and explore this complex creature’s fascinating characteristics.
--
Deep in the jungles of southeast Asia lurks a deadly creature with sharp teeth and a venomous bite. But it’s not a snake. It’s a slow loris, the only venomous primate, and one of only a few venomous mammals. Despite their wide-eyed looks, their story is much fiercer than many might imagine. Stephanie A. Poindexter dives into this complex creature’s surprising characteristics.
Lesson by Stephanie A. Poindexter, directed by Reza Riahi.
Support Our Non-Profit Mission ---------------------------------------------- Support us on Patreon: http://bit.ly/TEDEdPatreon Check out our merch: http://bit.ly/TEDEDShop ----------------------------------------------

item.title: Something Came Out of 3I/ATLAS
item.author: Astrum
item.content:encoded:
3I/ATLAS will soon be gone forever, and it just did the strangest thing yet. Buy the DWARF Mini & Mini Tripod and receive a free Mini Bag, while supplies last. Get yours here: https://bit.ly/4duBnX9
▀▀▀▀▀▀
3I/ATLAS is leaving the Solar System. Having flown past Jupiter and now rapidly receding into deep space, the interstellar object has scientists scrambling to make final observations before it is out of reach forever. In our fourth and final video about 3I/ATLAS, we're digging into the final images we might ever see of it, the latest discoveries from its loop around our Sun, and the mysterious explosion that could change everything we know.
Watch our previous videos about 3I/ATLAS here - Part 1: https://youtu.be/XEggZo3SXNM Part 2: https://youtu.be/bwJgcflf0lM Part 3: https://youtu.be/zk0YoiJDSSQ
▀▀▀▀▀▀
0:00 3I/ATLAS Is Leaving 1:35 Explosion 3:54 X-Ray Halo 7:22 3I/ATLAS’s Heartbeat 8:43 3I/ATLAS’s Size 10:35 Symmetrical Jets 11:42 Europa Clipper 13:43 JUICE 15:36 Parker Solar Probe 16:03 TESS 17:16 How Old Is 3I/ATLAS? 18:42 Where Did It Come From? 21:08 3I/ATLAS’s Past 22:50 Jupiter 24:14 The Next Interstellar Object
▀▀▀▀▀▀
To stay on top of space news, sign up to the Astrum newsletter: https://astrumspace.kit.com
A huge thanks to our Patreons who help make these videos possible. Sign-up here to support the channel: https://bit.ly/4aiJZNF

Join us on the Astrum discord: https://discord.gg/TKw8Hpvtv8
▀▀▀▀▀▀

Astrum Earth: https://youtube.com/@AstrumEarth Astrum Extra: https://www.youtube.com/@astrumextra
Astrum Spanish: https://www.youtube.com/@astrumespanol Astrum Portuguese: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UChn_-OwvV63mr1yeUGvH-BQ
▀▀▀▀▀▀
References: “SPHERE-X Tracks Brightening of 3I/ATLAS”, via nasa.gov https://astrumspace.info/spherex3i “X-Ray Halo of 3I/ATLAS”, via esa.int https://astrumspace.info/xrayhalo “Rotation Period of 3I/ATLAS”, via harvard.edu https://astrumspace.info/rotation3i “Nucleus and Post-perihelion Activity of 3I/ATLAS”, via iop.org https://astrumspace.info/nucleus3i “Europa Clipper Sees 3I/ATLAS in UV”, via seti.org https://astrumspace.info/euclipper3i “TESS Reobserves Comet 3I/ATLAS”, via nasa.gov https://astrumspace.info/tess3i “Isotopic Evidence of Distant Origin of 3I/ATLAS”, via arxiv.org https://astrumspace.info/3iorigin “Water D/H in 3I/ATLAS as a Probe of Formation Conditions”, via arxiv.org https://astrumspace.info/water3i “In Search of Witnesses to 3I/ATLAS’s Voyage”, via arxiv.org https://astrumspace.info/3ivoyage “ 3I/ATLAS Approach to Jupiter”, via mdpi.com https://astrumspace.info/3ijupiter
▀▀▀▀▀▀
Credits: Writer: Shourya Shrivastava Video Editor: Nathália Huzian Audio Editor: James Horsley Researcher: Shourya Shrivastava Script Editor: Damaris McColgan Thumbnail Designers: Peter Sheppard, Juane Dames Publishing Lead: Georgina Brenner Production Coordinator: Rose Unwin Edit Producer: Poppy Pinnock Head of Astrum: Jess Jordan Creator of Astrum: Alex McColgan
With special thanks to: NASA/ESO/ESA
#Astrum #Space #3IATLAS

item.title: How Lover's Lane Became A Terrifying Place
item.author: Weird History
item.content:encoded:
A young couple heads to Lovers Lane for a romantic night alone. But then a chilling radio alert interrupts their date: a dangerous escaped killer with a hook for a hand is on the loose nearby. Terrified, the teenagers rush home. Yet what they discover when they arrive is even more horrifying, a metal hook dangling from the car door handle.
In this episode of Weird History Presents: Graveyard Shift, we explore the shocking history behind America's most famous urban legend, the real life crimes that mirror that legend, and the unsettling connection between teenage romance, fear, and death.
Have you ever been scared to park in a lover's lane? Let us know in the comments.

item.title: Just how ancient is sumo? - Lee Thompson
item.author: TED-Ed
item.content:encoded:
Trace the history of sumo, and explore how the sport was designed and modified to reflect ancient Japan’s culture and traditions.
--
Sumo wrestling is known across the world as a beloved form of entertainment, a grand display of strength, and an ode to Japan's ancient past and traditions. But surprisingly, many of these so-called "ancient" traditions were actually recent inventions. So, what were these fabricated practices, and how do they relate to sumo’s true history? Lee Thompson traces the evolution of the sport.
Lesson by Lee Thompson, directed by Masayoshi Nakamura, Good General.
Support Our Non-Profit Mission ---------------------------------------------- Support us on Patreon: http://bit.ly/TEDEdPatreon Check out our merch: http://bit.ly/TEDEDShop ----------------------------------------------

Thank you so much to our patrons for your support! Without you this video would not be possible! item.title: Timeline 1969 - Everything That Happened In 1969
item.author: Weird History
item.content:encoded:
Have you ever wondered what it was like to live in the 1960s? Timeline is the Weird History channel's prestige documentary series that examines history, decade by decade and year by year, covering the people, news, culture, sports, and entertainment that defined the past and shaped the world we live in today.
In this episode we look at 1969, the year The Beatles broke up, the Manson Family started claiming victims, and mankind landed on the moon.
So which events from 1969 interest you the most? Let us know in the comments!
(Much like real history, this video was produced entirely by human beings.)
Be sure to subscribe to the Weird History Newsletter: https://bit.ly/WeirdHistoryNews Be sure to subscribe to the Weird History Newsletter: https://bit.ly/WeirdHistoryNews
#Timeline #1969 #WeirdHistory

item.title: The Dark Side of the World’s Lightest Structural Metal
item.author: Real Engineering
item.content:encoded:
Get 60% off some petty revenge: https://incogni.com/realengineering
Watch this video ad free on Nebula: https://nebula.tv/videos/realengineering-the-material-science-of-magnesium
Links to everything I do: https://beacons.ai/brianmcmanus
Get your Real Engineering shirts at: https://standard.tv/collections/real-engineering

Viktor Józsa, Ron Hochsprung

item.title: Oddities of the Victorian Era | Compilation
item.author: Weird History
item.content:encoded:
A lot has been written about the Victorian Era, and many films have been made focused on various aspects of life in those times. That said, today we are delving into some of the more obscur aspects of life in the Victorian era! All kinds of weird that may have never really made it to the silver screen, all for your curiosity! Enjoy! Let us know in the comments what your favorite aspect of Victoria life is!
Be sure to subscribe to the Weird History Newsletter: https://bit.ly/WeirdHistoryNews
Chapters: 00:00:00 -Unusual Facts You Never Knew About Queen Victoria 00:10:31 - Myths About The Victorian Era, Debunked 00:23:49 - Victorian Cannibalism Was Worse Than You Think 00:36:10 - Most Unusual Victorian Era Jobs 00:47:30 - Victorian Mourning Practices 00:58:04 - Smelly Facts About London's The Great Stink of 1858 01:08:18 - How Victorian-Era Britons Hired Child Gangs to Do Their Bidding 01:17:15 - All The Strange Things Found In Queen Victoria’s Coffin
#victorianera #compilation #weirdhistory

item.title: Why does catnip make cats go crazy? - Jaap de Roode
item.author: TED-Ed
item.content:encoded:
Explore the fascinating reason why cats go crazy for catnip, and what evolutionary purpose the plant served to feline species.
--
Of the many bewildering behaviors cats display, one of the strangest is their obsession with a specific species of plant. After just one whiff, even the most stoic cat can start pawing, drooling, biting, and wriggling in a state of pure feline euphoria. So, why do cats go crazy for catnip? Jaap de Roode shares the fascinating reason why this herb sends our feline friends into a frenzy.
Lesson by Jaap de Roode, directed by Héloïse Dorsan-Rachet.
Support Our Non-Profit Mission ---------------------------------------------- Support us on Patreon: http://bit.ly/TEDEdPatreon Check out our merch: http://bit.ly/TEDEDShop ----------------------------------------------

Thank you so much to our patrons for your support! Without you this video would not be possible! item.title: This Isn’t Sci-Fi. It’s the Future of Space Travel
item.author: Astrum
item.content:encoded:
The future of space travel might be closer than you think. If you love learning about science as much as I do, head to http://brilliant.org/astrum to learn for free for a full 30 days. You'll also receive 20% off a premium annual subscription, giving you unlimited access to everything on Brilliant.
▀▀▀▀▀▀
Could we actually unlock interstellar travel, or is it destined to stay in science fiction? ​​What technology will actually get us there? In this video, we’re diving into the mind-blowing tech currently in development - and the wild concepts still a way off - that could finally take us to the stars. Some of these will definitely surprise you.
▀▀▀▀▀▀
0:00 Space Travel 2:15 How Rockets Work 4:57 Ion Engines 10:19 Space Elevator 12:16 Nuclear Rockets 15:03 Solar Sails 17:33 Space Tethers 19:29 Warp Drive 22:15 Laser & Electron Beams
▀▀▀▀▀▀
To stay on top of space news, sign up to the Astrum newsletter: https://astrumspace.kit.com
A huge thanks to our Patreons who help make these videos possible. Sign-up here to support the channel: https://bit.ly/4aiJZNF
Astrum Displate Posters: https://displate.com/artist/astrumspace?art=5f04759ac338b Astrum Merch: https://astrum-shop.fourthwall.com/
Join us on the Astrum discord: https://discord.gg/TKw8Hpvtv8
▀▀▀▀▀▀
Astrum Podcast on Spotify: https://open.spotify.com/show/6jPRrbq3o3dpvBb173ZTKi?si=a90d3efe3b704c83
Astrum Earth: https://youtube.com/@AstrumEarth Astrum Extra: https://www.youtube.com/@astrumextra
Astrum Spanish: https://www.youtube.com/@astrumespanol Astrum Portuguese: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UChn_-OwvV63mr1yeUGvH-BQ
▀▀▀▀▀▀
References: “In-Space Propulsion Technology”, via nasa.gov https://astrumspace.info/inspaceprop “95 Years Ago: Goddard's First Liquid-Fueled Rocket”, via nasa.gov https://astrumspace.info/goddard “Basics of Spaceflight: Rocket Propellants”, via braeunig.us https://astrumspace.info/propellants “Electric Propulsion”, via spacepropulsion.mit.edu https://astrumspace.info/electricprop “Ion Propulsion”, via science.nasa.gov https://astrumspace.info/ionprop “The Space Elevator Construction Concept”, via obayashi.co.jp https://astrumspace.info/spaceelevator “Space Nuclear Propulsion”, via nasa.gov https://astrumspace.info/nuclearprop “NASA Next-Generation Solar Sail Boom Technology Ready for Launch”, via nasa.gov https://astrumspace.info/solarsail “An Unanticipated Intersection with the Alcubierre Warp Metric”, via link.springer.com https://astrumspace.info/warpmetric “Relativistic Electron Beam Could Propel Probe to Alpha Centauri”, via phys.org https://astrumspace.info/e-beam

With special thanks to: NASA/ESO/ESA
#Astrum #Space #Travel

item.title: The Depraved History Behind The REAL Natural Born Killers
item.author: Weird History
item.content:encoded:
In 1957, 19-year-old Charles Starkweather and his 14-year-old girlfriend, Caril Ann Fugate, embarked on one of America's most infamous killing sprees. Over two terrifying months the teenage couple murdered ten people across Nebraska and Wyoming, leaving a trail of fear and unanswered questions.
On this episode of Weird History Presents: Graveyard Shift, we take a look at the rampage which began with the murders of Fugate's parents and young half-sister before escalating into a series of seemingly random attacks that captivated the nation and went on to inspire Quentin Tarantino's script for the movie Natural Born Killers.
Have you seen Natural Born Killers? Let us know in the comments!
Be sure to subscribe to the Weird History Newsletter: https://bit.ly/WeirdHistoryNews
#naturalbornkillers #hollywood #backstory
